package.note: This is a field/panel photograph, not a schematic sheet — there is NO title block, drawing number, or sheet identifier visible. Content is a physically installed PLC and its terminal/wiring, read directly off the hardware.

devices.detail: Allen-Bradley Micro820 programmable controller (20-point). Nameplate reads 'AB Allen-Bradley', 'Micro820', catalog '2080-LC20-20QBB'.
Two/three light-gray modules mounted on the DIN rail to the right of the Micro820; no legible catalog or tag text visible. Function not determinable from photo.

terminals.evidence: Green terminal blocks with slotted screws above the controller face; blue conductors landed

terminals.detail: Lower green pluggable screw-terminal strip spanning under the modules; many slotted screws, blue and white/cream conductors landed. Terminal numbers not legible.

conductors.evidence: Blue insulated conductors entering terminal blocks

conductors.detail: White/cream jacketed multi-conductor cable(s) bundled with a cable tie, routed past the modules to the lower terminal strip. Purpose not identifiable from photo.
conductors.evidence: White/cream jacketed cable bundle with white zip tie

Front-panel output LED map labelled 0 1 2 3 / 4 5 6 under heading 'OUT' — 7 embedded output points/indicators.

cross_sheet_notes: No schematic sheets or cross-references are present in this image; it is a standalone hardware photograph. All designations are read directly from the device nameplate and front-panel silk-screen.

brief.sheet_title: Field photograph of an Allen-Bradley Micro820 PLC (catalog 2080-LC20-20QBB) mounted on a DIN rail with its green pluggable I/O terminal blocks wired — this is hardware, not a drawing sheet.
brief.purpose: The photo shows a Micro820 20-point programmable controller with its embedded I/O terminals wired with blue field conductors and a white/cream cable bundle. The controller's front face carries a diagnostic LED map: input indicators 0–11, output indicators 0–6, and status LEDs RUN, FAULT, FORCE, COMM, ENET and SD. It has an on-board Ethernet port (MAC ID 5C:88:16:D8:E4:D7). Several unmarked gray modules sit on the same DIN rail to the right. Because no schematic or wire/terminal markings are legible, individual signal assignments cannot be determined from this image alone.

brief.troubleshooting_example: If an input LED (say one of IN 0–11) does not light when the field device operates, isolate device-versus-wiring at two points: (1) at the sensor/field-device end, confirm the device actually switches its output; (2) at the corresponding green input terminal on the Micro820, confirm that same switched signal arrives. If the device switches but nothing arrives at the PLC input terminal, the fault is the wire/terminal between them; if the signal is present at the PLC terminal but the IN LED still does not turn on, the fault is in the input point/module. NOTE: this photo shows no schematic and no control logic, so it cannot tell you what the program does with that input — only the hardware indication is visible here.
brief.safety_context: The visible nameplate does NOT print an operating voltage, so this image does not establish the I/O voltage level — do not assume it. For continuity/resistance checks on the wiring or terminals, de-energize, lock out, and verify absence of voltage first. If you must verify a live input signal, treat it as energized work and use appropriately rated test equipment and PPE. A photograph does not prove present field conditions — confirm the actual state before contacting terminals.
